mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 12% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 12%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production above 1% indicates strengthening manufacturing activity, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO rises by >1% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 1.2 million units signals strong consumer demand for residential construction, driving equity market optimism and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1.2M units monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and housing starts indicates robust economic demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue prospects.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and HOUST both rise > 0.5% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST rise by >0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates a stagflationary environment, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 100k jobs signals improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly.

mechanism_narrative: High residential sales (RSAFS > 400k units) combined with low unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) indicates strong consumer demand and labor market health, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS exceeds 400k and UNRATE is below 5%.
